data.title: Criminals and Their Networks Hold $75B in Crypto Assets, Says Chainalysis
data.description:
Criminals and their downstream networks hold approximately $75 billion in crypto assets obtained through illicit means according to Chainalysis, with darknet market participants controlling over $46. 2 billion and illicit entity balances reaching $15 billion representing a 359% surge from 2020 levels.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/criminals-and-their-networks-hold-75b-in-crypto-assets-says-chainalysis/

data.title: Bitcoin Short-Term Prediction: Why The Price Will Cross $140,000 By The End Of October
data.description:
New Bitcoin (BTC) price forecasts suggest that the leading cryptocurrency could cross $140,000 before the end of October. Based on historical data and advanced empirical modeling, a crypto analyst has confirmed that the probability of Bitcoin finishing the month above this key level appears increasingly likely. Bitcoin Price Set For Major October Rally According to a price prediction shared by crypto analyst and economist Timothy Peterson on X social media, Bitcoin’s trajectory in October appears promising. His AI-based bootstrapped simulation chart also suggests that half of the month’s gains may have already been realised
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-price-will-cross-140000/

data.title: María Corina Machado Awarded 2025 Nobel Peace Prize for Democracy Efforts in Venezuela
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, María Corina Machado, a prominent human rights activist and political opposition leader in Venezuela, has been honored with the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize for her significant contributions to promoting democracy in her country. Machado has been a vocal advocate for Bitcoin, describing it as a 'lifeline' for Venezuelans facing economic challenges. In a 2024 interview with Alex Gladstein of the Human Rights Foundation, she highlighted Bitcoin's role in helping Venezuelans protect their wealth amidst hyperinflation and government-imposed exchange rates. Machado emphasized that Bitcoin has transitioned from a humanitarian tool to a crucial means of resistance, advocating for property rights, low inflation, and equal access to opportunities as essential for aiding Venezuela's poorest citizens.

Critics, however, have accused the Venezuelan opposition movement of being influenced by foreign interests. Reports indicate that five Venezuelan opposition leaders met with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio in May to discuss potential regime change strategies. Despite these criticisms, Bitcoin continues to be viewed as a vital resource for political activists and individuals in countries with hyperinflating currencies and strict currency controls. The peer-to-peer nature of Bitcoin allows these groups to preserve wealth and bypass state-imposed financial restrictions.

Peer-to-peer technology is increasingly recognized as a tool for political opposition movements worldwide. In 2022, Canadian trucker convoy protesters turned to Bitcoin after the government froze their bank accounts during demonstrations against COVID-19 restrictions. Although the Canadian government managed to seize some Bitcoin sent through centralized channels, the majority of the funds sent via decentralized methods remained out of reach. Similarly, in Nepal, protesters utilized Jack Dorsey's Bitchat application, a peer-to-peer encrypted messaging network, to communicate during a government-imposed social media ban. Bitchat's ability to function without internet reliance, using Bluetooth to create a communication mesh, proved invaluable during the internet blackout. These instances underscore the growing importance of decentralized technologies in supporting freedom and resistance against governmental control.
